topics: Need of CFD as an analysis tool
Applications of CFD. CFD software packages and tools. CFD solution procedure
post-processing. Classification of partial differential equations (PDE) and boundary conditions
well-posed problems. Review of governing equations
Finite difference approximation of a derivative
discretization of differential equations using finite differences
concept of explicit and implicit methods
Gauss elimination methods. Detailed study of Navier stokes Equation-Solution of the Navier Stokes Equations. Grid arrangement-collocated and staggered grid. SIMPLE algorithm
Need for turbulence modelling. Closure problem. Reynolds averaged Navier Stokes equation. Turbulence modelling. Multiphase flow modelling in CFD
Multiphase flow modelling approaches Eulerian-Lagrangian approach
Volume of fluid approach. Suggestions on Project Topics Assignment/Project
20 Marks Students should evaluate and analyze a real-world problem
provide a conclusion on which solution is most appropriate for the problem
and implement the chosen solution using any computational tools like ANSYS
Evaluate the clarity and completeness of the problem definition. Are the objectives well-defined? Is the problem broken down into manageable sub-problems?  Identification of Key Parameters (2 points
Assess the identification and justification of key parameters influencing the fluid dynamics problem. Are all relevant variables and boundary conditions considered? 2. Analytical Approach
Evaluate the soundness of the chosen analytical methods. Are the equations and principles correctly applied to the problem?  Critical Analysis (1 points
Assess the depth of critical analysis in the approach. Are assumptions and limitations clearly stated and justified? 3. Data Interpretation
Evaluate the accuracy and consistency of the data interpretation. Are the results logically derived from the data?  Comparison with Theoretical Predictions (2 points
Assess how well the results are compared with theoretical predictions or existing literature. Are discrepancies analyzed and explained? Level
Evaluate the appropriateness of the chosen CFD tool for the problem at hand. Is the tool well-suited to model the specific fluid dynamics scenario?  Simulation Accuracy and Robustness (2 points
Assess the accuracy and robustness of the simulation setup. Are the mesh
and convergence criteria appropriately chosen? 2. Results Validation
Evaluate the extent and rigor of validation against experimental or benchmark data. Are the validation methods appropriate and convincingly presented?  Error Analysis (1 point
Assess the thoroughness of error analysis. Are sources of error identified and quantified? Are their impacts on the results discussed? 3. Conclusions and Recommendations
Evaluate the logical consistency of the conclusions drawn from the analysis and simulation results. Are the conclusions supported by the data?  Practical Implications and Recommendations (1 point
Assess the practical implications and recommendations based on the findings. Are the recommendations feasible and well-grounded in the analysis? 4. Presentation and Communication
Evaluate the clarity and organization of the project report or presentation. Is the information logically structured and easy to follow?  Technical Writing and Visual Aids (2 points
Assess the quality of technical writing and the effectiveness of visual aids (graphs
simulations). Are complex concepts clearly explained and visually supported
